revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14894 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://one-plate.city.omuta.lg.jp/spots/detail/cfcb29e0-ea75-4066-a5b9-219b01bc5eae Omuta City Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Ōmuta, Fukuoka]]

The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14894 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://one-plate.city.omuta.lg.jp/spots/detail/cfcb29e0-ea75-4066-a5b9-219b01bc5eae Omuta City Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>

It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji ware]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji pottery]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14894 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://one-plate.city.omuta.lg.jp/spots/detail/cfcb29e0-ea75-4066-a5b9-219b01bc5eae Omuta City Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji pottery]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[Haji pottery]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Ōmuta Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ōmuta Station|Ōmuta Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]][[ja:潜塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109357768]] Wikidata name: Kugurizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.01611111111111|N|130.4511111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, [[:en:Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagi|Sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ōmuta Station|Ōmuta Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]][[ja:潜塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109357768]] Wikidata name: Kugurizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.01611111111111|N|130.4511111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, [[:en:Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagi|Sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ōmuta Station|Ōmuta Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14894 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://one-plate.city.omuta.lg.jp/spots/detail/cfcb29e0-ea75-4066-a5b9-219b01bc5eae Omuta City Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagi|Sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ōmuta Station|Ōmuta Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]]. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagi|Sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]], and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a dragon and tiger image [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], but the grave goods show a distinctive [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ōmuta Station|Ōmuta Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]][[ja:潜塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]][[ja:潜塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]][[ja:潜塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109357768]] Wikidata name: Kugurizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.01611111111111|N|130.4511111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, {{ill|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|en|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|WD=Q385623|lt=Ōmuta, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|en|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|WD=Q385623|lt=Ōmuta}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}}. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagi|Sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}, and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included a dragon and tiger image {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}, but the grave goods show a distinctive [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ōmuta Station|en|Ōmuta Station|WD=Q871063|lt=Ōmuta Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|en|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|WD=Q385623|lt=Ōmuta}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}}. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagi|Sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}, and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included a dragon and tiger image {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}, but the grave goods show a distinctive [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ōmuta Station|en|Ōmuta Station|WD=Q871063|lt=Ōmuta Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14894 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://one-plate.city.omuta.lg.jp/spots/detail/cfcb29e0-ea75-4066-a5b9-219b01bc5eae Omuta City Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:潜塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q109357768}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Ōmuta, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]][[ja:潜塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]][[ja:潜塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kugurizuka Kofun]][[ja:潜塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kugurizuka Kofun | native_name =潜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kugurizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.01611111111111|N|130.4511111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location =1-429 Koganecho, {{ill|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|en|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|WD=Q385623|lt=Ōmuta, Fukuoka|1=Ōmuta, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|00|58|N|130|27|04|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kugurizuka Kofun'''|潜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Koganecho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|en|Ōmuta, Fukuoka|WD=Q385623|lt=Ōmuta|1=Ōmuta, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== This Kugurizuka Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located on a small independent hill to the east of the city of Ōmuta; however, its original shape has been lost due to the surrounding area being turned into agricultural land and research in 2001 revealed the possibility that it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagi|Sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}, and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a dragon and tiger image {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, but the grave goods show a distinctive [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ōmuta Station|en|Ōmuta Station|WD=Q871063|lt=Ōmuta Station|1=Ōmuta Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211400|title=潜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>

The tumulus has a diameter of 30 meters and a height of 6.5 meters, but its original height is estimated to be about eight meters. As for the internal structure, two box-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagi|Sarcophagi]] were found in the center of the mound, arranged side by side with their main axes oriented roughly north–south during [[archaeological excavation]]s in 1965.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> No traces of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found.
The western sarcophagus is made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}, and is approximately 1.7 meters long, 0.4 to 0.5 meters wide, and 0.45 to 0.5 meters deep. It was surrounded by stones the size of a human head, and the inside was painted vermilion. Along with human bones, {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a dragon and tiger image {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, and two pipe balls. An iron sword, two iron daggers, and one iron sickle from outside the sarcophagus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The east sarcophagus is set 1.3 meters apart and is roughly the same size as the west sarcophagus, and is made of sandstone. It was built by demolishing part of the western sarcophagus, so it is a later burial. The style of the sarcophagus was typical of northern Kyushu dating back to the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, but the grave goods show a distinctive [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] influence. An antechamber was attached to the front end of the sarcophagus, and two {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} jars were placed near the south end. Inside the west sarcophagus, was bronze mirror fragments with inner flower patterns. Outside the sarcophagus, there were 47 bronze arrowheads, one hoe point, one sickle, two axes, two planes, some other iron fragments, and two Haji pottery pots.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From the grave goods it was estimated that this tumulus dates from the mid-5th century, although more recent research indicates that it may actually date from the early 4th century, or early Kofun period. Excavated items are displayed at the Ōmuta City Historical Museum.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located on a hill about 150 meters northeast of Harumakita Elementary School, approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ōmuta Station|en|Ōmuta Station|qid=Q871063|lt=Ōmuta Station|1=Ōmuta Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
